diagrams.title: Process State Transition Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows the five states of a process and all possible transitions between them
elements: Arrows showing transitions between states with labels
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how processes move between states and what triggers each transition - essential for understanding CPU scheduling and multiprogramming

diagrams.title: Process Control Block Structure
diagrams.description: Structure of PCB showing all fields stored for each process
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing what information the OS maintains for each process - helps understand how processes are managed and switched

diagrams.title: Context Switch Flow
diagrams.description: Step-by-step process of switching between two processes
elements: Save A's context (registers, PC) to A's PCB
Update A's state from Running to Ready
Select Process B from Ready Queue
Load B's context from B's PCB
Update B's state from Ready to Running
Start executing B from its PC
PCB A and PCB B in memory
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how the CPU transfers control between processes - reveals the overhead involved in multitasking
